extraction.one_line_summary: HIVERT Jonathan won from a late 3-rider break after ALAPHILIPPE shredded the peloton on the final climb, distancing pure sprinters.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE's attacks on Côte de Châbana dropped sprinters systematically, forcing HIVERT/SÁNCHEZ/DI GRÉGORIO break at 10km out.
Direct Energie exploited HIVERT's positioning rather than organizing chase, revealing team awareness that sprint chaos favors opportunism.
Yellow jersey DÉMARE lost 38 seconds by sitting in peloton rather than bridging, showing GC passivity risks on hilly finishes.

rider_signals.HIVERT Jonathan: positive: Fifth win already in 2018 season despite typical 2-3 annual victory rate, exceptional early form suggests prolonged peak
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Controlled final climb pace to eliminate sprinters while preserving GC position, tactical maturity beyond pure aggression
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: negative: Lost yellow jersey by 38 seconds through passive positioning when break escaped at 10km, hesitant on selective finishes
rider_signals.SÁNCHEZ Luis León: positive: Took yellow jersey via breakaway collaboration despite losing sprint, opportunistic GC positioning without team support
